maturity_physical.high: Person describes having reached full physical/bodily maturity (اكتمال النمو الجسدي) beyond just legal marriage age — full puberty, general health that allows fulfilling the physical demands and responsibilities of married life and parenthood (motherhood/fatherhood), for both the man and woman.
maturity_physical.medium: A middle ground between full physical/bodily maturity (complete growth, health fit for marital and parental physical demands) and merely having reached puberty or legal marriage age without full bodily/health readiness — the transcripts mainly discuss these two poles, so medium is interpolated as partial physical readiness.
maturity_physical.low: Person conflates physical maturity with simply reaching puberty or the legal age for marriage ('the body grew up so I'm ready'), without genuine completion of physical growth or health capacity to handle the physical responsibilities of marriage/parenthood, which the material explicitly criticizes as a common mistake in Arab societies.

maturity_psychological.high: Person shows ability to manage and understand their emotions, control anger/sadness without being overwhelmed, has psychological stability, can face pressures without collapsing, takes responsibility for their feelings and behavior instead of blaming others, expresses needs correctly, and accepts criticism and sees problems as learning opportunities.
maturity_psychological.medium: A middle ground between full psychological maturity (emotional control, stability, taking responsibility, accepting criticism) and psychological immaturity (letting anger/sadness dominate, blaming others, being unable to face pressure) — an interpolation since the transcripts describe these as opposite poles.
maturity_psychological.low: Person lets emotions like anger or sadness fully control them, cannot manage pressure without collapsing, blames people around them for their own feelings/actions, cannot express needs properly, and reacts defensively to criticism rather than learning from problems — the transcripts warn that any small conflict escalates into a major problem when this maturity is lacking.

maturity_social.high: Person can deal well and respectfully with family, friends, and acquaintances, understands their social roles (as spouse, parent, child, friend), maintains these relationships without letting them negatively affect the marriage, balances privacy of the home with social duties, and can handle in-laws' interference or external disputes calmly without shaking the household's stability.
maturity_social.medium: A middle ground between strong social maturity (respectful handling of family/in-law dynamics, balancing privacy and social duties, calm conflict management) and its absence (letting outside relationships or interference destabilize the marriage) — an interpolation between the poles the material discusses.
maturity_social.low: Person cannot handle relationships with in-laws, family, or friends respectfully, lets external interference or disputes shake the stability of the home, and fails to balance social duties with the privacy/boundaries of the marital relationship.

maturity_professional.high: Person has a stable source of income, however modest, and the ability to work and earn, enabling them to bear the material responsibilities toward their household and family even if living modestly at first.
maturity_professional.medium: A middle ground between having a stable, even if small, income and capacity to work (professional maturity) versus lacking any reliable means to bear marital financial responsibilities — an interpolation, as the transcripts frame this mainly as present/absent.
maturity_professional.low: Person has no stable source of income and no real capacity or plan to work and earn, leaving them unable to bear the material responsibilities of a household, which the material frames as a basic prerequisite that's missing.

maturity_intellectual.high: Person shows logical thinking, can analyze matters deeply, makes decisions after reflection rather than impulsively following any idea or opinion, and has a vision for life and marriage rather than living day-to-day; they can discuss calmly and find solutions to problems with a partner.
maturity_intellectual.medium: A middle ground between strong intellectual maturity (deep analysis, deliberate decisions, a life vision, calm problem-solving) and impulsive, unreflective thinking with no vision for life or marriage — an interpolation between the two poles described.
maturity_intellectual.low: Person acts impulsively, jumps behind any idea or opinion without thinking it through, has no vision for life or marriage beyond living day to day, and cannot make well-considered decisions or discuss problems calmly — described as lacking the intellectual maturity needed for the decisive decisions marriage requires.

emotional_hunger_risk.high: Person shows strong signs of choosing/staying in relationships from a place of 'emotional hunger' — intense, unconscious attraction to whoever fills an unmet need (attention, appreciation, safety), tolerating insults, control, or mistreatment out of fear of losing 'the only person who gives them this,' seeing only the need being met rather than the person's actual character, and describing themselves as unable to live/breathe without the other person (pathological attachment).
emotional_hunger_risk.medium: A middle ground between clear emotional hunger/neediness (unconsciously choosing partners to fill a deprivation-driven need, tolerating mistreatment) and entering relationships from a place of 'self-sufficiency/surplus' (filling one's own emotional cup first via connection with God, self-sufficiency, and a wide circle of loved ones) — an interpolation since the material frames this as a clear dichotomy.
emotional_hunger_risk.low: Person describes having filled their emotional needs already — through their relationship with God, self-sufficiency, and a wide circle of family/friends/colleagues who exchange feelings with them — entering relationships from a place of 'addition' rather than need, seeing a potential partner as a bonus rather than as someone who 'completes' or rescues them from deprivation.

need_understanding.high: Person emphasizes mutual understanding as essential — being understood, expressing themselves and letting the other express too, and being able to disagree respectfully while reaching common ground, without hurting each other.
need_understanding.medium: A middle ground between strong mutual understanding (expressing and being understood, disagreeing respectfully, reaching common ground) and its absence (no ability to express or comprehend each other) — an interpolation, since the transcripts present understanding mainly as a stated essential need without graded detail.
need_understanding.low: Person shows little or no ability/desire to express themselves or comprehend the other's perspective, cannot disagree without hurting the other, and lacks the drive to reach a common point of understanding described as essential to a relationship.

need_containment.high: Person values 'احتواء' (containment) strongly — wanting a partner who can reassure them when they're overwhelmed, hug them without needing explanation, try to understand their weakness, and stand beside them rather than against them, recognizing that not everything needs a solution, sometimes just a hug or a comforting word.
need_containment.medium: A middle ground between strong felt need for containment (comfort, being held/understood without words, someone standing beside you) and little to no such need or expression — an interpolation, as the transcripts describe containment mainly as a stated key need without graded detail.
need_containment.low: Person shows little value placed on being comforted/held or having their emotional overwhelm absorbed by a partner, and doesn't describe needing someone to simply be present or comfort them without demanding solutions.

need_attraction.high: Person clearly values attraction on multiple levels — not just physical/beauty, but psychological and intellectual attraction too — describing feeling that the partner satisfies them from within, feeling comfortable and content psychologically, intellectually, and physically together.
need_attraction.medium: A middle ground between full multi-dimensional attraction (physical, psychological, and intellectual satisfaction with the partner) and its absence — an interpolation, since the material presents attraction mainly as a described ideal need rather than a graded scale.
need_attraction.low: Person shows little or no sense of psychological, intellectual, or physical satisfaction/attraction toward the partner, describing the relationship as lacking that felt sense of being 'enough' or content with the other person on these levels.

need_companionship.high: Person strongly values 'الونس' (companionship/company) — feeling they're not alone even in silence, that silence with the partner isn't awkward but comforting, echoing the described Quranic concept of 'sakina' (tranquility) present even in tough moments, not just happy ones.
need_companionship.medium: A middle ground between strong felt companionship (comfortable silence, not feeling alone, sakina in all moments) and its absence (feeling isolated or that silence is awkward) — an interpolation, since companionship is described mainly as a stated ideal.
need_companionship.low: Person shows little sense of comfort or ease in shared silence or presence with a partner, feeling alone or that quiet moments together are uncomfortable rather than reassuring, missing the described 'sakina' companionship provides.

need_trust.high: Person emphasizes trust as central — feeling safe with the partner, able to confide weaknesses without fear of betrayal, feeling secure even amid disagreements, without suspicion of the other's fidelity or intentions.
need_trust.medium: A middle ground between strong trust (safety, ability to confide, security despite disagreements) and its absence (suspicion, fear of betrayal, inability to feel safe) — an interpolation between the two poles the transcripts describe.
need_trust.low: Person describes suspicion or doubt toward the partner, fear of betrayal, or discomfort confiding vulnerabilities, meaning — per the material — they cannot love peacefully or feel safe in the relationship.

need_belonging.high: Person feels a deep sense of belonging — that the partner feels like 'family, blood, one's own people,' not a stranger, describing a deeper bond than just being spouses.
need_belonging.medium: A middle ground between a strong sense of belonging (feeling the partner is like kin, a deep bond) and feeling like strangers to one another — an interpolation, since belonging is presented mainly as a described ideal rather than a graded scale.
need_belonging.low: Person feels like a stranger to the partner, lacking the described deeper bond of feeling the other is 'from your people/blood,' with the relationship remaining superficial rather than familial in feeling.

need_support.high: Person values having a partner who pushes them forward, who notices when they're struggling and helps carry them rather than just watching, who is happy for their success and wishes them well genuinely — likened to the Prophet's support of his wives (listening, being compassionate, bearing burdens).
need_support.medium: A middle ground between strong mutual support (actively helping, celebrating success, carrying burdens together) and its absence (being a passive bystander who doesn't help or share the load) — an interpolation between the described poles.
need_support.low: Person describes a partner (or themselves) as passive/uninvolved in hard times, not helping carry burdens or actively support the other's success, contrasting with the described ideal of a supportive partner who pushes you forward.

need_reassurance.high: Person describes feeling a strong sense of tranquility/security (الطمأنينة) from the relationship — the partner's presence reduces fear rather than increasing it, and the relationship doesn't feel like a psychological battlefield, source of worry, or tension.
need_reassurance.medium: A middle ground between strong felt reassurance (presence reduces fear, feeling safe and calm) and its absence (relationship as a source of anxiety or conflict) — an interpolation between the poles the transcripts describe.
need_reassurance.low: Person describes the relationship as increasing fear, worry, or tension rather than reducing it — resembling what the material calls a psychological battlefield rather than a source of calm and reassurance.
